blocks.content: They went inside and sat in the living room — the same room where they had eaten dinner two nights ago, laughed about the camping trip, raised their glasses. The candles Sophie had lit were still in the middle of the table, burned down to nothing now.
The deputy asked them to stay together and wait. Then he went back upstairs.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: His family. Someone needs to call his family.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: His mom, Dean. She needs to hear it from someone who knew him. She can't just—

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: Not yet. We wait until someone tells us we can.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: Because that's how this works. That's what we're supposed to do.
Sophie looked like she wanted to argue and didn't have the energy.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: He seemed fine this morning. You all saw — breakfast. He was laughing. He made the fish. He was completely fine.

blocks.speaker: Wren
blocks.content: He wasn't fine on the beach.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: He said he was tired. That's all. People get tired.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: That's not — being tired is not—

blocks.speaker: Wren
blocks.content: I know, Cade. I'm not saying it is.
Cade pressed his palms flat on the table. Breathed. Nodded.
